Underlying refusals (sourced from project-alpha-blocked)
==========================================================

These are the six Strict-Only gate refusals that emergency mode
bypasses in this fixture. Each refusal is computed by the
corresponding gate's normal evaluation; emergency mode does NOT
prevent the evaluation — only the halt.

# 1. direct-strategy-on-standard-project
   Source: config.yaml declares project_kind: standard AND git.strategy: direct.
   Step: 0 (prelude).

# 2. upstream-sync-unverified
   Source: .tl/tl-sync-verdict.json → verdict: "UNVERIFIED",
   workflow_detail: "wire-evidence-missing".
   Step: 2 (UC status gate prelude).

# 3. upstream-qa-unverified
   Source: .tl/tl-qa-aggregate.json → aggregate: "UNVERIFIED",
   missing_mandatory_evidence on UC-200, UC-300.
   Step: 2 (UC status gate prelude).

# 4. missing-prod-golden-path
   Source: .tl/tl-qa-aggregate.json → qa-stage:prod-golden-path: NOT_RUN
   on UC-200 (matrix marks mandatory).
   Step: 3b (deploy health check; W4 strict — HEALTH_ONLY is not a substitute).

# 5. graph-stale
   Source: .tl/graph-baseline-stale.json → baseline 970 nodes;
   live capture (simulated) returns 1083 nodes; +113 delta.
   Step: 7 (graph stamping pre-flight).

# 6. sa-validate-critical
   Source: .tl/sa-validate-report.json → status: "FAIL",
   critical_count: 1.
   Step: 7 (alongside graph evaluation).

Without emergency mode (and without any signed exception), the
release skill emits the FIRST refusal it encounters in step order
and halts. With emergency mode, every refusal is printed as a
bypass banner and the run advances to a (emergency-bypass)
terminal headline.
